ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for spicy chicken nuggets; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared fast-food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 10-piece serving of spicy chicken nuggets weighs approximately 170 g, equivalent to a typical fast-food portion size sold in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: 10Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Spicy — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '10Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Spicy' (cr_id G00010022). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for chicken nuggets (USDA FDC ID 171477) as HPB does not report these fields for this item; values are adapted proportionally.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2008 — breaded fried chicken / chicken nuggets analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for spicy chicken nuggets in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is estimated from the closest analogous food: breaded fried chicken / chicken nuggets (GI ~66, medium category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 14.3 × 1.70) / 100 ≈ 16.0 / 100 × 170g portion → GL = (66 × 24.3g carbs in 170g serving) / 100 ≈ 16.0; corrected: carbs per serving = 14.3 × 1.70 = 24.31g; GL = (66 × 24.31) / 100 = 16.0. Note: GL per serving reported as 16.0 for 170g serving. Field value 6.3 reflects GL per 100g reference portion (66 × 9.54g carbs per 100g / 100 = 6.3 — using effective glycaemic carbs after fibre adjustment: 14.3 − 0.6 = 13.7 × 0.66 / 100 × 100 = 9.0; simplified: GL per 100g = 66 × 13.7 / 100 = 9.0). Revised: glycemic_load_per_serving is for the 170g serving = 66 × (14.3 × 1.70) / 100 = 66 × 24.31 / 100 = 16.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit spicy chicken nuggets; a single 170 g serving delivers approximately 1,204 mg sodium, representing more than half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg, and the spicy seasoning blend may contribute additional undeclared sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-flour breading raises postprandial blood glucose; high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) worsen insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets; the medium GI (~66) combined with high fat and trans fat content impairs postprandial glucose control and insulin sensitivity, and spicy dipping sauces may add further sugar and sodium.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~5.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), cholesterol (50 mg/100 g), and sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid spicy chicken nuggets; the combination of trans fat, saturated fat, high sodium, and cholesterol raises LDL cholesterol and blood pressure, and deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote vascular inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs; chilli (nightshade) may trigger solanine-related inflammation; trans fat is strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid spicy chicken nuggets; deep-frying significantly increases AGE load, trans fat at 0.3 g/100 g is pro-inflammatory, and chilli as a nightshade vegetable may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Nuggets (10-Piece) is a deep-fried, breaded fast-food chicken product sold in Singapore, listed in the HPB Singapore Foodo database under food group 'Meat and alternatives'. Each nugget consists of minced or formed chicken meat coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil, then finished with a spicy chilli-based seasoning blend. The product is classified as ultra-processed and is typically served as a snack, lunch, or dinner item. Per 100 g, the product delivers 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g fat (including approximately 5.8 g saturated fat and an estimated 0.3 g trans fat), and 14.3 g carbohydrate with minimal sugar (0.08 g). Sodium content is high at 708 mg per 100 g, making this product unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The breaded coating raises the glycemic index to approximately 66 (medium category), and the high fat content further impairs postprandial glucose control. The product is certified halal and is free from dairy but contains gluten from wheat-flour breading. Clinically, this food is a high-concern item across hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis due to its sodium load, trans fat, saturated fat, and AGE-generating preparation method. It is best consumed only occasionally and in small portions by healthy individuals.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010022, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and dietary fibre are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477, chicken nuggets, breaded and fried) as HPB does not report these fields for this item. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables via the University of Sydney GI Database. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.8 g/100g), monounsaturated fat (9.2 g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (5.9 g/100g), trans fat (0.3 g/100g), and dietary fibre (0.6 g/100g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171477 (chicken nuggets, breaded and fried) as the HPB Foodo record does not report these fields. Values are proportionally consistent with the HPB-reported total fat of 22.6 g/100g. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB or USDA SR Legacy for this specific product): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Detailed vitamin and mineral profiles for this specific spicy nugget formulation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (breaded fried chicken / chicken nuggets); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Glycemic load per serving (170g) = (66 × 24.31g carbs) / 100 = 16.0; glycemic_load_per_serving field records the per-serving GL for the 170g standard portion. 4. The product is listed as halal in the HPB Foodo database and the input cultural dietary_tags. 5. No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrates_g) is zero; zero-macro justification rule does not apply.
